context: ASA:  greater curvature Pathophysiology:   disrupted mucosal barrier (H. pylori) with vulnerability to acid + secretion of large volume of gastric juice containing little acid Incidence:  5÷10,000; 100,000 annually (USA) Age peak:   55-65 years; M÷F = 1÷1 Multiplicity: (a)  multiple in 2-8% (17-24% at autopsy), especially in patients on aspirin (b)  coexistent duodenal ulcer in 5-64%; gastric÷duodenal = 1÷3 [1÷7] in adults [children] proximal half of stomach in older patients (geriatric ulcer); adjacent to GE junction within hiatal hernia √  ulcer size usually < 2 cm (range 1-250 mm); in 4% >
